--- Comment `@coderabbitai help` to get the list of available commands.

**6 issues found** across 16 files
Prompt for AI agents (unresolved issues) ```text Check if these issues are valid — if so, understand the root cause of each and fix them. If appropriate, use sub-agents to investigate and fix each issue separately. P2: The `addTime` test mutates the vendored SunCalc module's global `times` array, which creates a test ordering dependency. After `addTime(-4, "customDawn", "customDusk")` runs, every subsequent `getTimes()` call in the same process will unexpectedly include `customDawn` and `customDusk` keys. While the current describe-block ordering makes this benign, it's fragile — adding a test after this block or reordering the blocks would silently break isolation. Consider restoring the global state after the test (e.g., by saving and resetting the `times` array length) to keep tests hermetic. P2: The environment variables section claims all listed vars are validated at server startup via `lib/environment.ts`, but `REVALIDATE_SECRET` isn't in the zod schema. A missing `REVALIDATE_SECRET` won't fail at startup — it silently produces an undefined secret that yields 401 when the revalidation endpoint is called. Either add `REVALIDATE_SECRET` to the environment schema so it's validated at startup, or add a note to the docs clarifying it's validated at request time only. P2: The `toThrow()` assertions in three tests don't specify the expected error. Consider asserting with `toThrow(ZodError)` or checking the error message, so a regression that makes `getEnvironment()` throw for the wrong reason won't silently pass. P1: Missing edge-case coverage for all-null hourly observations: `calculateRanges([])` returns `Infinity` for min fields and `-Infinity` for max fields after spread on empty array. The `HourlyApiResponseSchema` allows `.nullable()` items, so a date where every hourly slot is null would produce corrupted ranges. As a test gap this is notable — adding a test case that exercises the all-null path would expose the downstream bug in `calculateRanges` and signal that the implementation needs a guard (e.g., early return of a zeroed Ranges object, or handling at the call site before ranges are consumed by the UI). P3: The `makeRangeItem` helper in `fetcher.test.ts` and the `makeObservation` helper in `mappers.test.ts` define structurally identical default values for the `RangeObservation` type (all 18 fields: `date`, `min_outTemp`, `avg_outTemp`, ... `max_solarrad` with identical defaults). If the `RangeObservation` interface ever changes — a field is added, removed, or its default needs updating — both helpers must be updated in sync, which is easy to miss. Consider extracting a shared `makeRangeObservation` test factory to a `lib/__test__/` helper module that both test files import, and prefer typing overrides as `Partial` for type safety. P3: Coverage include glob `app/**/*.ts` misses the `.tsx` entry points in `app/`. Both `app/layout.tsx` and `app/page.tsx` will be excluded from the coverage report. Update the glob to `app/**/*.{ts,tsx}` to match every TypeScript file in the app directory, consistent with how `components/` already includes `.tsx`. ```
**Tip**: cubic can generate docs of your entire codebase and keep them up to date. Try it [here](https://docs.cubic.dev/wiki/ai-wiki?utm_source=github).

P1: Missing edge-case coverage for all-null hourly observations: `calculateRanges([])` returns `Infinity` for min fields and `-Infinity` for max fields after spread on empty array. The `HourlyApiResponseSchema` allows `.nullable()` items, so a date where every hourly slot is null would produce corrupted ranges. As a test gap this is notable — adding a test case that exercises the all-null path would expose the downstream bug in `calculateRanges` and signal that the implementation needs a guard (e.g., early return of a zeroed Ranges object, or handling at the call site before ranges are consumed by the UI).

The `HourlyApiResponseSchema` allows `.nullable()` items, so a date where every hourly slot is null would produce corrupted ranges. As a test gap this is notable — adding a test case that exercises the all-null path would expose the downstream bug in `calculateRanges` and signal that the implementation needs a guard (e.g., early return of a zeroed Ranges object, or handling at the call site before ranges are consumed by the UI).</comment>
